__notes.UI_HEALTH_ENABLED: Master switch: enables/disables entire UI health monitoring system. When false, no frame checks occur.
__notes.UI_HEALTH_BLANK_AVG: Luminance threshold (0-255 scale). Frame flagged blank if avg brightness < this value. Detects black/very dark screens. Uses ITU-R BT.601: Y = 0.299×R + 0.587×G + 0.114×B
__notes.UI_HEALTH_BLANK_STD: Standard deviation threshold for pixel brightness uniformity. Frames must have BOTH avg < BLANK_AVG AND std < BLANK_STD to trigger blank detection. Filters out grainy darkness and startup loading screens.
__notes.UI_HEALTH_SAMPLE_INTERVAL_MS: Milliseconds between frame samples. Lower = faster detection but higher CPU. Time to detect black screen = CONSECUTIVE_BLANK_NEEDED × this value.
__notes.UI_HEALTH_STALE_AFTER_MS: Milliseconds of no frame signature change before marking stream stale. Detects frozen frames (same image for N milliseconds). Independent of black screen check.
__notes.UI_HEALTH_CONSECUTIVE_BLANK_NEEDED: How many consecutive black samples required to trigger restart. Prevents false positives from brief stalls. Formula: detection_time = this × SAMPLE_INTERVAL_MS
__notes.UI_HEALTH_COOLDOWN_MS: Milliseconds to wait after health alert before allowing next restart attempt. Prevents restart loops if stream keeps failing immediately.
__notes.UI_HEALTH_WARMUP_MS: Milliseconds to wait after attaching monitor before checking health. Allows stream initial buffering/startup without false triggers. Monitoring suppressed for this duration.
__notes.UI_HEALTH_MAX_ATTEMPTS: Maximum restart attempts before giving up. Set to 0 for infinite attempts (not recommended - may cause endless restart loops for permanently failed cameras).
